lines.text: The first table presents data on the antibacterial efficacy of various Bac. against several pathogens, with the columns labeled
Bac., T. vulgaris, T. caramanicus, Z. multiflora, Z. clinopodioides, Z. tenuior, and Control. Under S.

page.content: aureus, the values are 6.25, 0.78, 1.30, 12.50, 12.50, and 8, respectively. For E. coli, the values are 6.25, 1.56, 1.30, 16.66, 20.83, and 8. The S. typhimurium row shows 12.50, 2.60, 1.56, 50.00, 25.00, and 8. In the S. dysenteriae row, the values are 12.50, 0.78, 1.56, 12.50, 10.41, and 8. B. subtilis has values of 10.41, 1.56, 1.30, 16.66, 12.50, and 8. The S. epidermidis row lists 6.25, 1.04, 1.56, 12.50, 10.41, and 8. For MRSA, the values are 12.50, 2.08, 3.125, 12.50, 16.66, and 8, while P. aeruginosa shows 6.25, 1.30, 1.56, 12.50, 10.41, and 8. The second table outlines strategies from Resnicow (2000) and Kreuter (2003), detailing Surface structure with Peripheral strategies, Linguistic strategies, Evidential strategies, Deep structure with Constituent-involving strategies, and Sociocultural strategies.
